key_topics: Number: reading and ordering numbers to 1,000,000
Multiplying and dividing by 10, 100, 1000
Long multiplication: three-digit × two-digit numbers
Long division: dividing three-digit numbers by two-digit numbers
Prime numbers, factors, multiples, square numbers, cube numbers
Fractions: adding and subtracting fractions and mixed numbers with different denominators
Multiplying fractions by whole numbers
Fractions, decimals, and percentages: converting between all three
Ratio: understanding ratio notation, sharing in a ratio
Decimals: multiplying and dividing decimals by 10, 100, 1000
Negative numbers: calculating across zero
Geometry: angles in triangles and quadrilaterals (sum of interior angles)
3D shapes: nets, surface area
Translation, reflection, and rotation of 2D shapes
Co-ordinates in all four quadrants
Data: pie charts, scatter graphs (introduction)
competences: Multiply and divide three-digit numbers by two-digit numbers
Add and subtract fractions with different denominators
Convert fluently between fractions, decimals, and percentages
Use co-ordinates in all four quadrants
Calculate interior angle sums of polygons
typical_mistakes: Errors in long division (forgetting to bring down digits)
Adding denominators when adding unlike fractions
Not accounting for all four quadrants in co-ordinate work

key_topics: Reading: responding to a wide range of text types and genres
Literary analysis: themes, characterisation, authorial choices
Comparing texts: different versions, different authors, same theme
Reading non-fiction: evaluating arguments, identifying bias
Writing: narrative with complex characters and sub-plots
Journalistic writing: newspaper reports, interviews
Speeches and formal presentations
Balanced arguments: pros and cons, connectives for argument
Grammar: passive voice, modal verbs, conditionals
Punctuation: semi-colons, colons, parenthetical dashes
Extended vocabulary: etymology, morphology, register
competences: Analyse texts with reference to literary techniques
Write sustained narratives with complex structure
Use a range of punctuation including semi-colons and colons
typical_mistakes: Balanced arguments that are not actually balanced
Overlong introductions in news reports (burying the lede)

key_topics: Living things: classification of living things (kingdoms)
Micro-organisms: bacteria, viruses, fungi — useful and harmful
Reproduction: human reproductive system, adolescence
Forces: air resistance, water resistance, streamlining
Gravity and its effects on planets and moons
Space: Solar System, planets, day/night, seasons
Earth in space: Moon phases, tides
Properties and changes of materials: thermal conductors and insulators
competences: Classify living things using a classification key
Explain the role of micro-organisms in the environment
Describe the Solar System and explain day/night and seasons
typical_mistakes: Thinking the Sun moves around the Earth (geocentric error)
Confusing the cause of seasons (orbit tilt vs. distance from Sun)
